title: frailea pumila subsp. maior
intro: Frailea pumila subsp. maior, also known as the 'Greater Frailea', is a cactus species native to Uruguay and Argentina, characterized by its cylindrical stems and dense, fine spines. Traditionally, Frailea pumila subsp. maior has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections due to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, Frailea pumila subsp. maior is typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its effects.
